GE-IDENTIFIER-LENGTH: {{path}}: Invalid identifier `{{value}}`, length must be between {{min}} and {{max}} characters (inclusive)
GE-IDENTIFIER-CHARS: {{path}}: Invalid identifier `{{value}}`, can only contain alphanumeric characters and underscores, and must not start with a digit
GE-ENUM-EMPTY: {{path}}: Invalid enum definition, at least one entry is required
GE-FLOAT-MIN: {{path}}: Invalid float validation config, value for `min` must be a finite float
GE-FLOAT-MAX: {{path}}: Invalid float validation config, value for `max` must be a finite float
GE-FLOAT-NORANGE: {{path}}: Invalid float validation config, range has no valid values
GE-FLOAT-WMIN: {{path}}: Redundant float validation config, inclusive `min` matches the minimum allowed value
GE-FLOAT-WMAX: {{path}}: Redundant float validation config, inclusive `max` matches the maximum allowed value
GE-FLOAT-WONE: {{path}}: Float validation config only restricts to a single value ({{value}})
GE-FLOAT-INFINITE: {{path}}: Value can't be represented using a finite 64-bit floating point number
GE-FUZZYDATETIME-JSON: {{path}}: Invalid type, expected Int (fuzzy date time)
GE-FUZZYDATETIME-PRECISION: {{path}}: Fuzzy date time {{value}} uses precision-encoding value (for {{precision}}), which is not allowed here
GE-FUZZYDATE-PRECISION: {{path}}: Fuzzy date {{value}} uses precision-encoding value (for {{precision}}), which is not allowed here
GE-FUZZYTIME-PRECISION: {{path}}: Fuzzy time {{value}} uses precision-encoding value (for {{precision}}), which is not allowed here
GE-INT-MIN: {{path}}: Invalid int validation config, value for `min` is not within the safe range for JS integers
GE-INT-MAX: {{path}}: Invalid int validation config, value for `max` is not within the safe range for JS integers
GE-INT-NORANGE: {{path}}: Invalid int validation config, range has no valid values
GE-INT-WMIN: {{path}}: Redundant int validation config, `min` matches the minimum allowed value
GE-INT-WMAX: {{path}}: Redundant int validation config, `max` matches the maximum allowed value
GE-INT-WONE: {{path}}: Int validation config only restricts to a single value ({{value}})
GE-INT-OUTRANGE: {{path}}: Value {{value}} out of configured bounds ({{min}} to {{max}})
GE-LIST-MIN: {{path}}: Invalid minLength, should be greater than 0
GE-LIST-MAX: {{path}}: Invalid maxLength, should be greater than 0
GE-LIST-NORANGE: {{path}}: Invalid array length range, range has no valid lengths
GE-LIST-UNIQUETYPE: {{path}}: Unsupported element type for unique list (only String, Int and Enum are supported)
GE-LIST-WMIN: {{path}}: Redundant minLength, matches the default minimum length of 0
GE-LIST-WEMPTY: {{path}}: Array length validation only allows empty arrays
GE-LIST-OUTRANGE: {{path}}: Array length {{length}} out of configured bounds ({{min}}-{{max}})
GE-LIST-DUPLICATES: {{path}}: Array is not allowed to contain duplicate entries
GE-MAP-MIN: {{path}}: Invalid minSize, should be greater than 0
GE-MAP-MAX: {{path}}: Invalid maxSize, should be greater than 0
GE-MAP-NORANGE: {{path}}: Invalid map size range, range has no valid sizes
GE-MAP-WMIN: {{path}}: Redundant minSize, matches the default minimum size of 0
GE-MAP-WEMPTY: {{path}}: Map size validation only allows empty maps
GE-MAP-OUTRANGE: {{path}}: Map size {{size}} out of configured bounds ({{min}}-{{max}})
GE-OBJECT-DEFAULT-UUIDV4TYPE: {{path}}: GenerateUuidV4 default value can only be applied to UUID type
GE-OBJECT-DEFAULT-NOWDATETIMETYPE: {{path}}: NowDateTime default value can only be applied to fuzzy date time type
GE-OBJECT-DEFAULT-NOWDATETYPE: {{path}}: NowDate default value can only be applied to fuzzy date type
GE-OBJECT-DEFAULT-NOWTIMETYPE: {{path}}: NowTime default value can only be applied to fuzzy time type
GE-OBJECT-MISSINGPROP: {{path}}: Missing required property `{{prop}}` (has no default value)
GE-OBJECT-EMBEDROOT: {{pat}}: Embedding an object that extends a root entity is not allowed (object `{{object}}` extends root entity `{{entity}}`)
GE-OBJECT-BASEENTITYREF: {{path}}: Can't find definition for base entity `{{entity}}`
GE-OBJECT-BASEENTITYEXTENDABLE: {{path}}: Base entity `{{entity}}` is not extendable and cannot be used as a base for a custom object definition
GE-OBJECT-WBASEENTITYPROP: {{path}}: Base entity `{{entity}}` already defines a property `{{prop}}` which is redefined in the extension. The original property value will be hidden in the extended entity.
GE-OBJECT-EXTENDBUILTINPROPNOTOBJ: {{path}}: The extended builtin entity ({{entity}}) property {{prop}} does not refer to an object type and cannot be extended
GE-OBJECT-EXTENDBUILTINPROPEXTENDABLE: {{path}}: The extended builtin entity ({{entity}}) property {{prop}} does not refer to non-extendable object type and cannot be extended ({{ref}})
GE-OBJECT-EXTENDBUILTINPROPDEFREF: {{path}}: Can't find object definition for reference `{{ref}}` used to extend builtin entity ({{entity}}) property {{prop}}
GE-OBJECT-EXTENDBUILTINPROPDEFREFBUILTIN: {{path}}: Object definition `{{ref}}` does not extend the expected builtin entity type for property {{prop}} of builtin entity ({{entity}})
GE-OBJECT-EXTENDBUILTINPROPNOTFOUND: {{path}}: Property {{prop}} not found in builtin entity ({{entity}}) and cannot be extended
GE-STRING-MIN: {{path}}: Invalid minLength, should be greater than 0
GE-STRING-MAX: {{path}}: Invalid maxLength, should be greater than 0
GE-STRING-NORANGE: {{path}}: Invalid string length range, range has no valid lengths
GE-STRING-WMIN: {{path}}: Redundant minLength, matches the default minimum length of 0
GE-STRING-WEMPTY: {{path}}: String length validation only allows empty strings
GE-STRING-OUTRANGE: {{path}}: String length {{length}} out of configured bounds ({{min}}-{{max}})
GE-UUID-FORMAT: {{path}}: Value `{{value}}` is not a UUID respecting the format {{format}}
GE-CONFIG-EXT-ROOTREF: {{path}}: Invalid root extension, object definition `{{ref}}` not found
GE-CONFIG-EXT-ROOTBASE: {{path}}: Invalid root extension, object definition `{{ref}}` must be an extension for {{expectedBaseEntity}}
GE-CONFIG-EXT-EMBEDDEDBASE: {{path}}: Invalid extension for {{embeddedEntityName}}, object definition `{{ref}}` must be an extension for {{embeddedEntityName}}
GE-CONFIG-DUPID: {{path}}: Duplicate type identifier `{{id}}` found in configuration (used {{count}} times)
GE-BUILTIN-EXTENSIONNOTALLOWED: {{path}}: Extensions for builtin type at this path are not allowed.
GEM-EXT-REQUIRED: {{path}}: The extensions configuration or related object definitions changed, a migration for ({{sourceRefType}}) {{sourceRef}} -> ({{targetRefType}}) {{target}} is required.
GEM-PROV-DEFAULT: {{path}}: The configuration for this property requires to use the target entity default value but the target entity has no default value or has a non-constant default value.
GEM-PROV-SOURCE-MISSING: {{path}}: The configured migration specifies to use the value of a non-existing source property ({{sourcePropLocation}}) {{sourceProp}}.
GEM-PROV-SOURCE-WNOTNULLABLE: {{path}}: The configured mapped null value is unused, since the source property ({{sourcePropLocation}}) {{sourceProp}} is not nullable.
GEM-PROV-SOURCE-NULLABLE: {{path}}: The source property ({{sourcePropLocation}}) {{sourceProp}} is nullable but the target property is not; you must specify a value to use for the target when the source is nullable.
GEM-PROV-SOURCE-TRANSFORM: {{path}}: The source property ({{sourcePropLocation}}) {{sourceProp}} does not match the target type, you must provide an appropriate builtin value transformer or use a custom migration method.
GEM-PROV-SOURCE-TRANSFORMRECOMMEND: {{path}}: The source property ({{sourcePropLocation}}) {{sourceProp}} does not match the target type, you must provide an appropriate builtin value transformer (such as {{recommendation}}) or use a custom migration method.
GEM-PROV-SOURCE-APPLICABILITY: {{path}}: The requested value transformation ({{transform}}) from source property ({{sourcePropLocation}}) {{sourceProp}} is not applicable. Check other error messages for details.
GEM-ENUM-DUPLICATES: {{path}}: Duplicate enum migrations for {{source}} (builtin={{builtinSource}}) -> {{target}} (builtin={{builtinTarget}}), multiple definitions for the same source->target enum pair are not allowed.
GEM-ENUM-SOURCEREF: {{path}}: Enum definition for source reference `{{ref}}` does not exist.
GEM-ENUM-TARGETREF: {{path}}: Enum definition for target reference `{{ref}}` does not exist.
GEM-ENUM-MAPPING: {{path}}: Enum migration configuration does not define an explicit mapping for source entry `{{entry}}` and there is no applicable fallback.
GEM-ENUM-MAPPINGAUTO: {{path}}: Enum migration configuration does not define an explicit mapping for source entry `{{entry}}` and there is no applicable fallback, but could be mapped by enabling automatic mapping of entries with identical names in the target configuration.
GEM-ENUM-NULLABLE: {{path}}: Enum migration configuration is used with a non-nullable target, can't use null target value ({{mappingSource}}).
GEM-ENUM-BUILTINFALLBACK: {{path}}: Enum migration configuration requires a fallback value when the source enum is builtin
GEM-OBJECT-DUPLICATES: {{path}}: Duplicate object migrations for ({{sourceRefType}}) {{sourceRef}} -> ({{targetRefType}}) {{target}}, multiple definitions for the same source->target object pair are not allowed.
GEM-OBJECT-WUNUSEDSOURCE: {{path}}: Object migration does not use the value of the custom source property `{{prop}}` in configured builtin migrations. Unless this value is used in a custom migration function the content will be permanently lost during migration.
GEM-OBJECT-SOURCEREF: {{path}}: Object definition for source custom object reference `{{ref}}` does not exist.
GEM-OBJECT-TARGETREF: {{path}}: Object definition for target custom object reference `{{ref}}` does not exist.
GEM-OBJECT-MAPPING: {{path}}: Object migration configuration does not define a mapping for source property `{{prop}}` and configured fallbacks are not applicable. You must define a mapping for this property or add an appropriate fallback behavior
GEM-EXACT-TYPE: {{path}}: source and target have different type.
GEM-EXACT-NULLABLE: {{path}}: source and target have different nullability.
GEM-EXACT-CONFIG: {{path}}: source and target configurations are the same type but have different validation rules or configurations.
GEM-EXACT-ENUM: {{path}}: source and target enum definitions do not match exactly and there is no explicit migration.
GEM-EXACT-OBJECT: {{path}}: source and target object definitions do not match exactly and there is no explicit migration.
GEM-EXACT-OBJECT-PROPKEYS: {{path}}: source and target object definitions have different sets of property keys.
GEM-EXACT-LISTNONINJ: {{path}}: source and target cannot be an exact match; the list requires unique values but the enum element migration is non-injective and may introduce duplicate values after migration.
GEM-EXACT-MAPKEYNONINJ: {{path}}: source and target cannot be an exact match; the enum key migration is non-injective and may cause key collisions after migration.
GEM-TRANSFORM-WRAPLIST-TARGET: {{path}}: WrapToSingletonList transformer requires the target type to be a list.
GEM-TRANSFORM-WRAPLIST-SIZE: {{path}}: WrapToSingletonList transformer is incompatible with the target list size validation, which must allow lists of one element.
GEM-TRANSFORM-WRAPMAP-TARGET: {{path}}: WrapToSingletonMap transformer requires the target type to be a map.
GEM-TRANSFORM-WRAPMAP-SIZE: {{path}}: WrapToSingletonMap transformer is incompatible with the target map size validation, which must allow maps of one entry.
GEM-TRANSFORM-CLAMP-SOURCETYPE: {{path}}: ClampToRange transformer requires the source type to be a numeric type (Int or Float).
GEM-TRANSFORM-CLAMP-TARGETTYPE: {{path}}: ClampToRange transformer requires the target type to be a numeric type (Int or Float).
GEM-TRANSFORM-CLAMP-ROUNDING: {{path}}: ClampToRange transformer from a Float source to an Int target requires a rounding mode to be configured.
GEM-TRANSFORM-CLAMP-WNOEFFECT: {{path}}: ClampToRange transformer has no effect because the target range already covers the entire source range.
GEM-TRANSFORM-CLAMP-WCOLLAPSE: {{path}}: ClampToRange transformer will collapse all source values to a single boundary value, because the source and target ranges do not overlap.
GEM-TRANSFORM-SCALE-SOURCETYPE: {{path}}: ScaleToRange transformer requires the source type to be a numeric type (Int or Float).
GEM-TRANSFORM-SCALE-TARGETTYPE: {{path}}: ScaleToRange transformer requires the target type to be a numeric type (Int or Float).
GEM-TRANSFORM-SCALE-ROUNDING: {{path}}: ScaleToRange transformer with an Int target requires a rounding mode to be configured.
GEM-TRANSFORM-SCALE-WSAMERANGE: {{path}}: ScaleToRange transformer has no effect because the source and target ranges are identical.
GEM-TRANSFORM-ROUNDING-SOURCETYPE: {{path}}: Rounding transformer requires the source type to be Float.
GEM-TRANSFORM-ROUNDING-TARGETTYPE: {{path}}: Rounding transformer requires the target type to be Int.
GEM-TRANSFORM-ROUNDING-RANGE: {{path}}: The source Float range cannot be fully represented in the target Int range after rounding.
GEM-TRANSFORM-SLICESTRING-SOURCETYPE: {{path}}: SliceString transformer requires the source type to be String.
GEM-TRANSFORM-SLICESTRING-TARGETTYPE: {{path}}: SliceString transformer requires the target type to be String.
GEM-TRANSFORM-SLICESTRING-MINLEN: {{path}}: SliceString transformer cannot guarantee the target minLength constraint: the source minLength is smaller than the target minLength, so sliced strings may be too short.
GEM-TRANSFORM-SLICESTRING-WNOEFFECT: {{path}}: SliceString transformer has no effect because the target maxLength is undefined or greater than or equal to the source maxLength.
GEM-TRANSFORM-TLIST-SOURCETYPE: {{path}}: TransformList transformer requires the source type to be a list.
GEM-TRANSFORM-TLIST-TARGETTYPE: {{path}}: TransformList transformer requires the target type to be a list.
GEM-TRANSFORM-TLIST-MINLEN: {{path}}: TransformList transformer cannot guarantee the target minLength constraint: the target minLength is greater than the source minLength.
GEM-TRANSFORM-TLIST-SLICING: {{path}}: TransformList transformer: the target maxLength is smaller than the source maxLength but no slicing behavior is configured. You must specify how to handle lists that exceed the target size.
GEM-TRANSFORM-TLIST-WSLICENOOP: {{path}}: TransformList transformer: the configured slicing behavior will never trigger because the target maxLength is not smaller than the source maxLength.
GEM-TRANSFORM-TLIST-WDEDUP: {{path}}: TransformList transformer: the target requires unique values, duplicate entries will be silently discarded.
GEM-TRANSFORM-TLIST-WDEDUPT: {{path}}: TransformList transformer: the target requires unique values, if the provided element transformer is not injective some entries might be silently discarded.
GEM-TRANSFORM-TMAP-SOURCETYPE: {{path}}: TransformMap transformer requires the source type to be a map.
GEM-TRANSFORM-TMAP-TARGETTYPE: {{path}}: TransformMap transformer requires the target type to be a map.
GEM-TRANSFORM-TMAP-MINSIZE: {{path}}: TransformMap transformer cannot guarantee the target minSize constraint: the target minSize is greater than the source minSize.
GEM-TRANSFORM-TMAP-WSLICE: {{path}}: TransformMap transformer: the target maxSize is smaller than the source maxSize; map entries may be silently dropped.
GEM-TRANSFORM-TMAP-WDEDUP: {{path}}: TransformMap transformer: the key transformation may not be injective, which could cause key collisions and silent data loss.
GEM-TRANSFORM-TLIST-NULLELEM: {{path}}: TransformList transformer: the source list has a nullable element type but no nullElementMapping is configured; you must specify one or set the target element type to also be nullable.
GEM-TRANSFORM-TLIST-WNULLNOTNULLABLE: {{path}}: TransformList transformer: nullElementMapping is configured but the source element type is not nullable, so it will never be used.
GEM-TRANSFORM-TMAP-NULLVALUE: {{path}}: TransformMap transformer: the source map has a nullable value type but no nullValueMapping is configured; you must specify one or set the target value type to also be nullable.
GEM-TRANSFORM-TMAP-WNULLNOTNULLABLE: {{path}}: TransformMap transformer: nullValueMapping is configured but the source value type is not nullable, so it will never be used.
GEM-COERCE-NULLABLE: {{path}}: CoerceType cannot coerce a nullable type to a non-nullable type in this context. Nullable-to-non-nullable coercion is only supported in a FromSource property provider, where null values are handled by mappedNullValue.
GEM-COERCE-TYPE: {{path}}: CoerceType cannot coerce the source type to the target type; the two types are incompatible.
GEM-COERCE-NUMRANGE: {{path}}: CoerceType cannot coerce the source numeric type to the target numeric type; the source range is not fully covered by the target range.
GEM-COERCE-STRINGVALIDATION: {{path}}: CoerceType cannot coerce the source String to the target String; the target validation is more restrictive than the source.
GEM-COERCE-ENUM-REF: {{path}}: CoerceType cannot resolve one or both enum definitions for implicit enum coercion.
GEM-COERCE-ENUM-ENTRIES: {{path}}: CoerceType cannot coerce the source enum to the target enum; the target enum does not contain all entries of the source enum.
GEM-COERCE-OBJECT-BUILTIN: {{path}}: CoerceType cannot coerce different builtin objects.
GEM-COERCE-OBJECT-REF: {{path}}: CoerceType cannot perform implicit object coercion; one or both object definitions could not be resolved.
GEM-COERCE-OBJECT-BASEENTITY: {{path}}: CoerceType cannot perform implicit object coercion; source and target object definitions do not extend the same base entity.
GEM-COERCE-OBJECT-BADCOERCE: {{path}}: CoerceType cannot perform implicit object coercion; the source and target object definitions are not compatible with the configured fallback behaviors and no explicit migration is configured.
GEM-COERCE-OBJECT-PROP: {{path}}: CoerceType cannot map this target object property using any of the configured fallback behaviors.
GEM-COERCE-OBJECT-EXTBUILTINPROP: {{path}}: CoerceType cannot coerce to the target object because a builtin property of the extended object is extended in incompatible ways between source and target configuration.
GEM-COERCE-LISTSIZE: {{path}}: CoerceType cannot coerce the source list to the target list; the target size validation is more restrictive than the source.
GEM-COERCE-LISTUNIQUE: {{path}}: CoerceType cannot coerce the source list to the target list; the target requires unique values but the source does not guarantee uniqueness.
GEM-COERCE-LISTNONINJ: {{path}}: CoerceType cannot coerce the source list to the target list; the target requires unique values but the element type coercion is non-injective and may introduce duplicates.
GEM-COERCE-MAPVALIDATION: {{path}}: CoerceType cannot coerce the source map to the target map; the target size validation is more restrictive than the source.
GEM-COERCE-MAPKEYNONINJ: {{path}}: CoerceType cannot coerce the source map to the target map; the map key coercion is non-injective and may cause key collisions.
GEM-FALLBACK-DEFAULTOVERRIDE: {{path}}: Default value on target property cannot be used as fallback because it would override the corresponding value in the source property. If you want to allow overriding for all target properties with a default value add the `UseTargetDefaultIgnoringSource` fallback behaviour, or specify an explicit mapping for this property with `Use.TargetDefault`.
GEM-FALLBACK-NODEFAULT: {{path}}: Target property has no default value, or has a non-constant default value, can't apply default-based fallback behaviour.
GEM-FALLBACK-NOSOURCE: {{path}}: Target property does not have matching source property, can't apply source-based fallback behaviour.
